ticket.raw_subject:
Hi PH MultiCarrier Support Team,

I am reaching ou...
ticket.description:
Hi PH MultiCarrier Support Team,

I am reaching out regarding a shipping rate issue on my Shopify Plus store (silverpalaceinc.com) that I have been unable to resolve on my own.

ISSUE SUMMARY
For certain product variants, UPS shipping rates do not appear at checkout. Only "Local Pickup" is shown. This affects specific variants across multiple products, while other variants on the same products work perfectly fine.

PRODUCTS AFFECTED
The issue is consistent across at least two products:
- 925 Sterling Silver Rope Chain or Bracelet — variants added after the initial product setup (rows 97+ in our product CSV), which includes all Rhodium, Gold, and Rose Gold plated variants, as well as some heavier Basic Non-Plated variants
- 925 Sterling Silver Figaro Chain or Bracelet — same behavior, variants added later (rows 105+) do not show shipping rates

WHAT I HAVE CONFIRMED
- The app is properly connected as a carrier service in Shopify's General shipping profile
- I have already removed and re-added the PH MultiCarrier rate in Shopify's shipping settings — the issue persists
- All affected variants are marked as physical products with "Requires Shipping" checked
- All affected variants have weight, correct fulfillment service, and identical shipping settings to the working variants
- When I test a working variant alone at checkout, UPS rates appear correctly
- When I test a broken variant alone at checkout, only Local Pickup appears
- When I add BOTH a working and a broken variant to the same cart, UPS rates disappear for the entire cart — meaning the broken variant is blocking rates for all items

ROOT CAUSE THEORY
Based on my testing, I believe the affected variants are not registered in the app's internal variant registry. They were added to existing products after the app was initially configured, and it appears the app did not automatically pick them up. Re-connecting the carrier service on Shopify's side did not resolve this, which suggests the sync needs to happen from within the app itself.

WHAT I NEED
Could you please:
1. Check whether there is a way to re-sync or re-index all current product variants from within the app
2. If not, could your team run a sync on your end for our store?
3. Confirm whether this is a known issue with variants added after initial setup and if there is a permanent fix

Our store is on Shopify Plus and we have a large number of products with many variants, so a reliable sync process is important for us going forward.

Please let me know if you need any additional information, order IDs, or specific variant details to investigate.

Thank you for your help.

Best regards,
Silver Palace Inc.
silverpalaceinc.com
info@silverpalaceinc.com
213-488-9906

------------------
Submitted from: https://mcsl-s.pluginhive.io/home/views/orders/status/allorders

Hello,



Update on this issue



https://silverpalaceinc.com/products/925-sterling-silver-figaro-chain-or-bracelet?variant=47971095052542



CH273-8 GP

CH274-8 GP



When those SKU are added to the cart it does not show a shipping method upon checkout. I am noticing that SKU's that were included in multi variant listing are poisoning the carts and thus not showing shipping methods



Can we have fix on this as its been very hard to keep calling customers to ask for shipping methods



Regards,

comments.body:
L3 - Case related to Product Variants not getting imported automatically.

The customer is constantly adding the variants to shopify and those are not getting imported to the app and due to this the shipping rates are not provided at the checkout.

I have recorded a video showing the case and have attached the recording for reference.

Could you check and let us know how to handle this case and what steps to be taken moving forward?

Store URL - silver-palace-jewelry.myshopify.com
App - Shopify MCSL
Account ID - fe5f5f11-5fea-48f5-931e-4055928f88b1
comments.html_body:
L3 - Case related to Product Variants not getting imported automatically. 
 
The customer is constantly adding the variants to shopify and those are not getting imported to the app and due to this the shipping rates are not provided at the checkout. 
 
I have recorded a video showing the case and have attached the recording for reference. 
 
Could you check and let us know how to handle this case and what steps to be taken moving forward?
